---
title: "Baysian model"
output: pdf_document
date: "2026-04-17"
---

```{r}
# Bayesian-style PMF in R

set.seed(42)

# 1. Load data
train_path <- "train_ratings.csv"
val_path   <- "val_ratings.csv"
test_path  <- "test_ratings.csv"

train_df <- read.csv(train_path)
val_df   <- read.csv(val_path)
test_df  <- read.csv(test_path)

cat("Train shape:", dim(train_df), "\n")
cat("Val shape:", dim(val_df), "\n")
cat("Test shape:", dim(test_df), "\n")

head(train_df)


# 2. Basic checks
total_n <- nrow(train_df) + nrow(val_df) + nrow(test_df)

cat("Train proportion:", nrow(train_df) / total_n, "\n")
cat("Val proportion:", nrow(val_df) / total_n, "\n")
cat("Test proportion:", nrow(test_df) / total_n, "\n")

cat("Train rating min/max:", min(train_df$rating), max(train_df$rating), "\n")
cat("Val rating min/max:", min(val_df$rating), max(val_df$rating), "\n")
cat("Test rating min/max:", min(test_df$rating), max(test_df$rating), "\n")


# 3. Build mappings
all_user_ids  <- unique(c(train_df$user_id, val_df$user_id, test_df$user_id))
all_movie_ids <- unique(c(train_df$movie_id, val_df$movie_id, test_df$movie_id))

n_users <- length(all_user_ids)
n_items <- length(all_movie_ids)

cat("Number of users:", n_users, "\n")
cat("Number of movies:", n_items, "\n")

user2idx <- setNames(seq_along(all_user_ids), all_user_ids)
movie2idx <- setNames(seq_along(all_movie_ids), all_movie_ids)

idx2user <- setNames(all_user_ids, seq_along(all_user_ids))
idx2movie <- setNames(all_movie_ids, seq_along(all_movie_ids))

map_df <- function(df) {
  df$u_idx <- as.integer(user2idx[as.character(df$user_id)])
  df$i_idx <- as.integer(movie2idx[as.character(df$movie_id)])
  df
}

train_df <- map_df(train_df)
val_df   <- map_df(val_df)
test_df  <- map_df(test_df)

head(train_df)


# 4. Helpers
clip_rating <- function(x) {
  pmin(5, pmax(1, x))
}

rmse <- function(actual, pred) {
  sqrt(mean((actual - pred)^2))
}


# 5. Train PMF model
# Model:
# r_hat_ui = mu + b_u + b_i + p_u^T q_i
fit_bayesian_style_pmf <- function(train_df, val_df, test_df,
                                   n_users, n_items,
                                   k = 20, epochs = 10,
                                   lr = 0.01, reg = 0.02, seed = 42) {
  set.seed(seed)

  global_mean <- mean(train_df$rating)

  # latent factors
  P <- matrix(rnorm(n_users * k, mean = 0, sd = 0.1), nrow = n_users, ncol = k)
  Q <- matrix(rnorm(n_items * k, mean = 0, sd = 0.1), nrow = n_items, ncol = k)

  # biases
  bu <- rep(0, n_users)
  bi <- rep(0, n_items)

  history <- data.frame(
    epoch = integer(),
    train_rmse = numeric(),
    val_rmse = numeric()
  )

  for (ep in 1:epochs) {
    order_idx <- sample(1:nrow(train_df))

    for (idx in order_idx) {
      u <- train_df$u_idx[idx]
      i <- train_df$i_idx[idx]
      r <- train_df$rating[idx]

      pred <- global_mean + bu[u] + bi[i] + sum(P[u, ] * Q[i, ])
      err <- r - pred

      # update biases
      bu[u] <- bu[u] + lr * (err - reg * bu[u])
      bi[i] <- bi[i] + lr * (err - reg * bi[i])

      # update latent factors
      pu_old <- P[u, ]
      qi_old <- Q[i, ]

      P[u, ] <- P[u, ] + lr * (err * qi_old - reg * pu_old)
      Q[i, ] <- Q[i, ] + lr * (err * pu_old - reg * qi_old)
    }

    # predictions
    train_pred <- global_mean +
      bu[train_df$u_idx] +
      bi[train_df$i_idx] +
      rowSums(P[train_df$u_idx, , drop = FALSE] * Q[train_df$i_idx, , drop = FALSE])

    val_pred <- global_mean +
      bu[val_df$u_idx] +
      bi[val_df$i_idx] +
      rowSums(P[val_df$u_idx, , drop = FALSE] * Q[val_df$i_idx, , drop = FALSE])

    train_pred <- clip_rating(train_pred)
    val_pred <- clip_rating(val_pred)

    train_rmse_val <- rmse(train_df$rating, train_pred)
    val_rmse_val   <- rmse(val_df$rating, val_pred)

    history <- rbind(
      history,
      data.frame(epoch = ep, train_rmse = train_rmse_val, val_rmse = val_rmse_val)
    )

    cat(sprintf("Epoch %d | Train RMSE: %.4f | Val RMSE: %.4f\n",
                ep, train_rmse_val, val_rmse_val))
  }

  # test rmse
  test_pred <- global_mean +
    bu[test_df$u_idx] +
    bi[test_df$i_idx] +
    rowSums(P[test_df$u_idx, , drop = FALSE] * Q[test_df$i_idx, , drop = FALSE])

  test_pred <- clip_rating(test_pred)
  test_rmse_val <- rmse(test_df$rating, test_pred)

  # counts for uncertainty
  user_counts <- rep(0, n_users)
  item_counts <- rep(0, n_items)

  for (u in train_df$u_idx) user_counts[u] <- user_counts[u] + 1
  for (i in train_df$i_idx) item_counts[i] <- item_counts[i] + 1

  # seen items
  user_seen <- split(train_df$i_idx, train_df$u_idx)
  user_seen <- lapply(user_seen, unique)

  model <- list(
    global_mean = global_mean,
    bu = bu,
    bi = bi,
    P = P,
    Q = Q,
    history = history,
    test_rmse = test_rmse_val,
    user_counts = user_counts,
    item_counts = item_counts,
    user_seen = user_seen,
    k = k,
    epochs = epochs,
    lr = lr,
    reg = reg,
    seed = seed
  )

  return(model)
}


# 6. Fit default model
model <- fit_bayesian_style_pmf(
  train_df = train_df,
  val_df = val_df,
  test_df = test_df,
  n_users = n_users,
  n_items = n_items,
  k = 20,
  epochs = 10,
  lr = 0.01,
  reg = 0.02,
  seed = 42
)

cat("\nFinal validation RMSE:", tail(model$history$val_rmse, 1), "\n")
cat("Test RMSE:", model$test_rmse, "\n")


# 7. Plot training curve
plot(model$history$epoch, model$history$train_rmse,
     type = "b", pch = 19, col = "blue",
     ylim = range(c(model$history$train_rmse, model$history$val_rmse)),
     xlab = "Epoch", ylab = "RMSE",
     main = "Bayesian-style PMF Training Curve")
lines(model$history$epoch, model$history$val_rmse,
      type = "b", pch = 19, col = "red")
legend("topright", legend = c("Train RMSE", "Validation RMSE"),
       col = c("blue", "red"), lty = 1, pch = 19)


# 8. Single prediction
predict_mean <- function(model, raw_user_id, raw_movie_id, user2idx, movie2idx) {
  if (!(as.character(raw_user_id) %in% names(user2idx))) return(NULL)
  if (!(as.character(raw_movie_id) %in% names(movie2idx))) return(NULL)

  u <- user2idx[as.character(raw_user_id)]
  i <- movie2idx[as.character(raw_movie_id)]

  pred <- model$global_mean +
    model$bu[u] +
    model$bi[i] +
    sum(model$P[u, ] * model$Q[i, ])

  pred <- clip_rating(pred)
  return(pred)
}

# example
sample_user <- train_df$user_id[1]
sample_movie <- train_df$movie_id[1]

pred <- predict_mean(model, sample_user, sample_movie, user2idx, movie2idx)
cat("Sample user_id:", sample_user, "\n")
cat("Sample movie_id:", sample_movie, "\n")
cat("Predicted rating:", pred, "\n")


# 9. Bayesian-style uncertainty
predict_with_uncertainty <- function(model, raw_user_id, raw_movie_id,
                                     user2idx, movie2idx,
                                     n_samples = 300, temp = 0.60) {
  pred <- predict_mean(model, raw_user_id, raw_movie_id, user2idx, movie2idx)
  if (is.null(pred)) return(NULL)

  u <- user2idx[as.character(raw_user_id)]
  i <- movie2idx[as.character(raw_movie_id)]

  user_count <- model$user_counts[u]
  item_count <- model$item_counts[i]

  var_u <- 1 / (1 + user_count)
  var_i <- 1 / (1 + item_count)
  scale <- 0.20 + 0.45 * sqrt(var_u + var_i)

  draws <- rnorm(n_samples, mean = pred, sd = scale)
  draws <- clip_rating(draws)

  stars <- 1:5
  weights <- sapply(stars, function(s) {
    exp(-0.5 * ((s - draws) / temp)^2)
  })

  row_sums <- rowSums(weights)
  weights <- weights / row_sums
  rating_probs <- colMeans(weights)

  list(
    mean_pred = mean(draws),
    low = as.numeric(quantile(draws, 0.025)),
    high = as.numeric(quantile(draws, 0.975)),
    rating_probs = rating_probs,
    draws = draws
  )
}

pred_info <- predict_with_uncertainty(
  model, sample_user, sample_movie, user2idx, movie2idx, n_samples = 300
)

cat("Predicted mean rating:", pred_info$mean_pred, "\n")
cat("95% interval:", pred_info$low, pred_info$high, "\n")
cat("Rating probabilities:\n")
print(data.frame(rating = 1:5, probability = pred_info$rating_probs))

barplot(pred_info$rating_probs,
        names.arg = 1:5,
        col = "skyblue",
        main = "Probability of Each Discrete Rating",
        xlab = "Rating", ylab = "Probability")


# 10. Top-N recommendation
recommend_top_n <- function(model, raw_user_id, user2idx, idx2movie, top_n = 10) {
  if (!(as.character(raw_user_id) %in% names(user2idx))) return(NULL)

  u <- user2idx[as.character(raw_user_id)]

  seen <- model$user_seen[[as.character(u)]]
  if (is.null(seen)) seen <- integer(0)

  scores <- model$global_mean +
    model$bu[u] +
    model$bi +
    as.vector(model$Q %*% model$P[u, ])

  scores <- clip_rating(scores)

  candidate_idx <- setdiff(1:length(scores), seen)
  candidate_scores <- scores[candidate_idx]

  ord <- order(candidate_scores, decreasing = TRUE)[1:min(top_n, length(candidate_scores))]

  rec_df <- data.frame(
    movie_id = as.numeric(idx2movie[as.character(candidate_idx[ord])]),
    predicted_rating = candidate_scores[ord]
  )

  return(rec_df)
}

rec_df <- recommend_top_n(model, sample_user, user2idx, idx2movie, top_n = 10)
print(rec_df)


# 11. Results summary
results_summary <- list(
  model = "Bayesian-style PMF (R version)",
  seed = 42,
  input_features = "user_id + movie_id only",
  task = "explicit movie rating prediction",
  metric = "RMSE",
  prediction_clip = "[1,5]",
  latent_dim = 20,
  learning_rate = 0.01,
  regularization = 0.02,
  epochs = 10,
  final_val_rmse = as.numeric(tail(model$history$val_rmse, 1)),
  test_rmse = as.numeric(model$test_rmse)
)

print(results_summary)
```
